summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the 'Weapons' series with a message on the spiritual weapon of confidence. He began by establishing the context of spiritual warfare from 2 Corinthians 10:4 and Ephesians 6:12, explaining that our daily battles are spiritual and require divine weapons. Pastor Travis defined confidence as a powerful state of mind that determines victory or defeat, using the biblical example of the twelve spies sent to survey the Promised Land. He contrasted the 'grasshopper mentality' of the ten fearful spies with the 'God-sized mentality' of Joshua and Caleb, emphasizing that our perspective on ourselves reveals our view of God. The core of the message was that true confidence is not self-generated but flows from our identity in Christ. Pastor Travis outlined four reasons believers can be confident: because we are God's unique creation, fearfully and wonderfully made; because we are incomparable and should stop the destructive habit of comparing ourselves to others; because we are called to live to please God, not people; and because we have the power of the Holy Spirit dwelling within us as His temple. He closed with the transformation of Peter, who moved from fear to bold, Spirit-empowered preaching after Pentecost, illustrating how the indwelling Holy Spirit is the ultimate source of godly confidence. The sermon concluded with a prayer for God to instill this divine confidence in every listener.
mainPoints: True confidence is a spiritual weapon rooted not in ourselves but in who God is and our identity as His unique creation.
We must break the destructive habit of comparing ourselves to others and live to please God, not people, to walk in freedom and purpose.
The ultimate source of our confidence is the indwelling power of the Holy Spirit, which transforms us and enables bold, victorious living.

versesMentioned.text: The weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world. On the contrary, they have divine power to demolish strongholds.

For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.
You made all the delicate, inner parts of my body and knit me together in my mother’s womb. Thank you for making me so wonderfully complex! Your workmanship is marvelous—how well I know it.
For we are God’s masterpiece. He has created us anew in Christ Jesus, so we can do the good things he planned for us long ago.
We do not dare to classify or compare ourselves with some who commend themselves. When they measure themselves by themselves and compare themselves with themselves, they are not wise.
Charm is deceptive, and beauty is fleeting; but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.

Hezekiah son of Ahaz began to rule over Judah in the third year of King Hoshea’s reign in Israel... He did what was pleasing in the LORD’s sight, just as his ancestor David had done... He remained faithful to the LORD in everything, and he carefully obeyed all the commands the LORD had given Moses. So the LORD was with him, and Hezekiah was successful in everything he did.
Fearing people is a dangerous trap, but trusting the LORD means safety.

Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God? You are not your own; you were bought at a price. Therefore honor God with your bodies.
And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies because of his Spirit who lives in you.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 139:14 (NIV) - 'I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well.'
Monday.action: Write down three things you appreciate about how God uniquely made you—your personality, a talent, or a physical feature.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for creating me with intention and purpose. Help me to see myself today as Your wonderful workmanship.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 10:12 (NIV) - 'We do not dare to classify or compare ourselves with some who commend themselves... they are not wise.'
Tuesday.action: When you catch yourself comparing your life, looks, or success to someone else today, intentionally thank God for one blessing in your own life.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, free me from the trap of comparison. Help me find my worth in You alone and celebrate the path You have for me.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 29:25 (NLT) - 'Fearing people is a dangerous trap, but trusting the LORD means safety.'
Wednesday.action: Make one decision today based solely on what you believe pleases God, not on what others might think.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, my desire is to be a God-pleaser, not a people-pleaser. Give me the courage to live for Your approval above all else.

Thursday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 6:19 (NIV) - 'Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, whom you have received from God?'
Thursday.action: Do one thing to honor your body as God's temple—this could be a healthy choice, resting, or avoiding an unhealthy environment.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, You dwell in me. Show me how to honor You with my body, my choices, and my environment today.

Friday.scripture: Romans 8:11 (NIV) - 'And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you...'
Friday.action: Spend five minutes in quiet prayer, consciously acknowledging the presence and power of the Holy Spirit within you.
Friday.prayer: God, thank You that the same power that raised Christ lives in me. I draw my confidence and strength from Your Spirit within me today.

Saturday.scripture: Ephesians 2:10 (NLT) - 'For we are God’s masterpiece. He has created us anew in Christ Jesus, so we can do the good things he planned for us long ago.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on one 'good thing' God has planned for you to do. Take one small, practical step toward it.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I am Your masterpiece, created for good works. Fill me with God-sized confidence to step into the purpose You have for me.
